summary_en: 4,464,200 shares acquired for ¥8,499M via ToSTNeT-3 on 2025-12-05. Acquisition exceeded distributable surplus by ¥2,923M (distributable surplus at execution: ¥5,576M). Third-party committee of external counsel established to investigate causation and remediation; no repayment demanded of shareholders. FY3/26 and FY3/27 dividend guidance unchanged.
title_en: Notification regarding establishment of third-party committee relating to acquisition of treasury shares in excess of distributable surplus
translation_en_brief: SENKO Group Holdings disclosed on 21 May 2026 that a treasury share acquisition completed on 5 December 2025 inadvertently exceeded the company's distributable surplus under the Companies Act and Accounting Rules. The acquisition involved 4,464,200 shares purchased for approximately ¥8,499 million via ToSTNeT-3 (open-market single-price execution). The distributable surplus at the time of execution was ¥5,576 million, meaning the acquisition price exceeded this limit by approximately ¥2,923 million. The excess was discovered during reconfirmation of the distributable surplus following publication of FY3/26 results. In response, the board established a third-party committee comprising three external counsel (from TMI General Law Offices) to investigate the factual circumstances, clarify the cause, and recommend remedial measures and safeguards against recurrence. The company made clear it is not demanding repayment or other compensation from share sellers. Financial statement impact and remedial actions remain under review; however, FY3/26 and FY3/27 dividend guidance remain unchanged.
